description: BEGINNER EXAMPLE - a 3-segment tendon-driven continuum robot (TDCR) mounted on a Franka arm. Segments are 'coupled': each segment's tendons route through every link below it (pull a tip tendon and the whole stack bends). Generate it with:  python generate.py --config example_three_segment_franka --franka
_comment_links: links_per_segment: how many rigid links approximate each flexible segment. More links = smoother bending but slower simulation (8-15 is typical).
_comment_lengths: segment_lengths: physical length of each segment, in METERS (here 3 x 0.1 m = 0.3 m total). Segments may have different lengths.
_comment_radius: radius: VISUAL radius of each link cylinder, in meters (cosmetic only - structural stiffness comes from material_properties.outer_radius below).
_comment_mass: mass: total backbone mass in kg. Only used by joint_config_mode 'direct'; in 'material' mode (below) per-link mass is derived from density x cross-section.
_comment_joints_per_link: joints_per_link: 2 = bending about two axes (the usual choice); 3 also adds axial twist.
_comment_joint_mode: joint_config_mode: 'material' derives joint stiffness from Young's modulus + cross-section via beam theory. Switch to 'direct' to give stiffness/damping numbers explicitly instead.
_comment_material: material_properties (used when joint_config_mode == 'material'): outer_radius/inner_radius set the STRUCTURAL backbone cross-section that determines stiffness - a small outer_radius means a flexible rod. density x that cross-section sets per-link mass. youngs_modulus in Pascals. damping is per-joint damping.
_comment_joint_range: joint_range / vert_joint_range: per-joint angular limits in degrees (bending joints / the axial joint).
_comment_actuation_mode: actuation_mode: 'parallel_tendons' = cable-driven (the standard TDCR). Other modes: 'direct_torque', 'position', 'velocity', 'none'.
_comment_segments: actuation_details.segments: one entry per segment. number_of_tendons: 3 (triangular layout) or 4 (cross/X). distance_to_backbone: tendon offset from the centerline in meters - bigger means more bending torque per unit of cable pull.

actuator_properties._comment_ctrl: tendon_ctrlrange: min/max commanded tendon-length change, in meters.
actuator_properties._comment_type: tendon_actuator_type: 'position' (length control, most common) or 'motor' (direct force).
actuator_properties._comment_kp: tendon_kp: position gain (how stiffly the cable tracks its commanded length). tendon_kp_scaling 'inverse_length' scales the gain so longer tendons aren't over-stiff.
actuator_properties._comment_pre: tendon_pretension: baseline cable tension (N) that holds the robot taut at rest. All motions are relative to this pretensioned state.
actuator_properties._comment_forcerange: tendon_forcerange: cables can only PULL, so force is clamped <= 0 (negative = pulling).

_comment_seg_offsets: seg_offsets: rotate each segment's tendon pattern about the backbone (radians). Staggering them (0, 30 deg, 60 deg) lets stacked segments bend in independent directions.
_comment_selfcol: disable_self_collision: true skips link-vs-link contact (faster, and avoids jitter for a slender backbone).
_comment_mounting: mounting: where the TDCR base sits. franka_* (used with --franka) is the base pose in the Franka end-effector frame; world_* (used with --world) is the base pose in the world.
